title: Investigate and reduce TUI render flicker
task:
There is visible flicker in the norn-tui render. The user reports 'a little bit of flicker going on' — undescribed beyond that, so this task is exploratory: identify the source, fix the highest-impact contributor. Likely culprits are (a) writes that are not bracketed by synchronized output (DEC private mode 2026) and so present mid-paint to the user, (b) erase-then-write sequences where the erase is visible for one tick before the write lands, (c) the full-panel redraw running too often when only a subset changed.

OUTCOME — the user experiences a steady, non-flickering TUI:
- The input panel does not visibly redraw when the user is just typing characters into a single line.
- The fixed panel separator, status bar, and streaming indicator do not flicker between agent events.
- Autocomplete popup open/close transitions look instantaneous (no half-painted intermediate state).
- Scroll-region content does not visibly redraw when only the fixed panel changed.
- Where flicker is unavoidable due to terminal capability gaps (e.g. terminals without DEC mode 2026), the impact is bounded and not visible at human-perception speeds.

requirements.description: Audit the redraw path and identify every write that is not wrapped in synchronized output (sync_with_guard in app/helpers.rs, or sync_render in render/style.rs). Produce a short structured list in the task report's `concerns` field: file:line site, what it writes, whether wrapping it in sync would be correct and feasible. Do not blindly wrap everything — synchronized output is a real terminal mode and writes to the scroll region (CO7) are NOT cursor-addressed and likely should not be sync-bracketed individually; what matters is fixed-panel paints.
requirements.acceptance: A concrete catalogue (in the task's structured-output concerns array, or written to docs/reports/tui-flicker-audit.md and committed) listing the sites. Even if no fixes land in this brief, the audit deliverable lets the next pass act surgically.

requirements.description: Fix the highest-impact unwrapped paint site identified in R1. The most likely candidate is the input-area render in app/render.rs::render_input_frame — that paints multiple visual rows one after another with erase-line escapes between, and a non-sync terminal will show the erase for one scan-line before the write lands. Wrap render_input_frame's per-row write loop AND the cursor-park write in a single sync_with_guard call so the whole frame is atomic on capable terminals. Verify the existing tests still pass — sync_with_guard injects sync markers around the body but the body's byte output is unchanged.
requirements.acceptance: render_input is wrapped in synchronized output on Kitty/sync-capable terminals. The render unit tests (render_paints_visual_rows_and_cursor, render_paints_multiline_wrap_rows) still pass. On a non-sync terminal the fallback is identical to today's behaviour. cargo test -p norn-tui passes.

requirements.description: Investigate whether the fixed-panel redraw runs on every event or only when needed. In crates/norn-tui/src/app/render.rs the redraw_panel function (and redraw_all that wraps it) gets called from multiple sites in event_loop.rs. If a single InsertChar fires redraw_all, that's overkill — the input area changed but the agent status panel, streaming indicator, popup, and status bar did not. If redraw_panel does redraw everything every call, propose (in the structured output's concerns) a fine-grained-redraw plan: which sites should call a hypothetical redraw_input_only instead of redraw_all. Do NOT implement the fine-grained split in this task — it's a larger change that needs design. Just propose.
requirements.acceptance: A proposal in the concerns array or in docs/reports/tui-flicker-audit.md identifying which call sites currently full-redraw when they could partially redraw, and which subsystem changes (panel components, indicator, popup, status bar) actually require a full panel paint.

requirements.description: Add (or extend) a tracing instrumentation that counts panel paints per second. Use a tracing::debug! line in redraw_panel that emits a counter the implementer can tally from logs. This is non-functional code that helps measure subsequent improvements; keep it gated to debug level.
requirements.acceptance: A debug log line in redraw_panel emits per call. cargo test passes; cargo clippy clean.

context:
FILES:
- crates/norn-tui/src/app/render.rs — redraw_all, redraw_panel, render_input, render_input_frame.
- crates/norn-tui/src/app/helpers.rs — sync_with_guard helper. Wraps a body in DEC mode 2026 begin/end pair on terminals that advertise the capability; on others, the body is bracketed by cursor hide/show as a degraded fallback. Description at line 148.
- crates/norn-tui/src/render/style.rs — sync_render at line 175. Similar primitive, different audience.
- crates/norn-tui/src/app/event_loop.rs — every redraw_all/redraw_panel/render_input call site.
- crates/norn-tui/src/render/fixed_panel.rs — FixedPanel::render, which paints all panel components in one pass with cursor-addressed writes.
- crates/norn-tui/src/terminal/caps.rs — caps for synchronized output detection (look for sync field).

DESIGN CONSTRAINTS (from DESIGN.md):
- CO7: scroll-region writes never use cursor addressing — they only append. Do NOT sync-wrap scroll-region writes.
- CO8: the fixed panel is the only cursor-addressed rendering — these ARE the candidates for sync wrapping.

WHAT'S ALREADY CORRECT — DO NOT REIMPLEMENT:
- The sync_with_guard / sync_render helpers themselves
- The DEC 2026 capability detection
- The cursor hide/show fallback for non-sync terminals

DO NOT GUESS — INSTRUMENT. R1 is an audit, not a refactor. R2 is the smallest concrete fix. R3/R4 are research deliverables for a larger follow-up brief; produce a clear proposal, do not start implementing a fine-grained redraw rewrite in this dispatch.

STANDARDS — per CLAUDE.md: no unwrap/expect/panic/todo; pedantic clippy clean; no TODO/FIXME/HACK; tests must still pass.

VERIFICATION: cargo check -p norn-tui; cargo clippy -p norn-tui --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p norn-tui.
